phase1_analyze.summary: Suite 00 contains 90 cases (FRR-001–FRR-090) in LEGACY 11-column format. The suite was NOT migrated to the enriched 15-column agent-native format. Key findings: (1) All steps lack [NAV]/[ACT]/[WAIT] tags — they use plain numbered prose. (2) No Business_Rule, Edge_Case_Refs, Test_Data, Cross_Layer_Checks, Failure_Signals, or Cleanup columns. (3) One old route found: /account/orders (line 57). (4) One hardcoded card number found: 4622943127013705 (FRR-034) — acceptable as it is an environment-specific test card, not a secret. (5) COV-2026-03-21-1550 added 131 cases to suites 02, 03, 04c, 13; high-priority scenarios from ORDERS, QUOTES, B2B-ORG, ADMIN-IMPERSONATION domains are NOT yet represented in Suite 00 master suite. (6) Five BL-* invariants have no corresponding Suite 00 case.
phase1_analyze.formatAssessment: LEGACY 11-column (ID,Title,Section,Type,Priority,Estimate,Preconditions,Steps,Expected Result,References,Automation Status). Must be migrated to 15-column enriched format.

oldRoutesFound.newRoute: /account/orders (no change needed — this is the protected page path, not an auth route)
oldRoutesFound.action: KEEP — /account/orders is the protected page, not a login route

routeAudit.note: No /register or /account/login routes found. The auth routes /sign-up and /sign-in are referred to by label ('Sign Up', 'Sign In') not URL, so no route updates needed for auth flows.

coverageGaps.description: Suite 00 uses 11-column legacy TestRail format. All 90 cases lack: Business_Rule, Edge_Case_Refs, Test_Data, Cross_Layer_Checks, Failure_Signals, Cleanup columns. Steps use numbered prose without [NAV]/[ACT]/[WAIT] tags. This makes the suite incompatible with agent-native execution.
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P0 — agents cannot execute these cases deterministically without tags
coverageGaps.action: MIGRATE — convert header to 15-column; rewrite steps with tags; add missing columns

coverageGaps.description: COV-2026-03-21-1550 added RMA/Return request (ORD-037–040), Invoice PDF download (ORD-041–043), Order cancellation (ORD-044–047), and Order confirmation depth (ORD-048–051) to suite 04c. None of these scenarios are represented in the Suite 00 master suite. Suite 00 only has FRR-043 (reorder) and FRR-044 (status state machine) for Orders.
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P1 — Return/RMA and Invoice are revenue-impacting customer-facing features
coverageGaps.action: GENERATE — add 2 new P1 cases: Return/RMA request flow, Invoice PDF download

coverageGaps.description: COV-2026-03-21-1550 added 13 quote cases to suite 04c (QUO-009–021). Suite 00 only has FRR-052 (happy path quote flow). Missing: quote expiry enforcement (BL-B2B-003 covered in 04c QUO-013), quote negotiation round-trip, quote rejection by buyer.
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P1 — Quote expiry prevents order at expired price (revenue protection)
coverageGaps.action: GENERATE — add 1 new P1 case: Quote expiry enforcement (BL-B2B-003)

coverageGaps.description: COV-2026-03-21-1550 added impersonation cases to suite 02. Suite 00 has no impersonation case at all. Admin impersonation ('Login on behalf') is a critical support/B2B feature.
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P1 — missing master suite coverage of a key B2B support capability
coverageGaps.action: GENERATE — add 1 new P1 case: Admin impersonation end-to-end

coverageGaps.description: BL-B2B-004 (delegated purchasing limits / order approval) has no coverage in Suite 00. This is a P0-revenue invariant. FRR-053 covers role visibility but not the approval workflow when a buyer exceeds their purchasing limit.
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P0 — orders exceeding buyer limit must enter approval state (revenue control)
coverageGaps.action: GENERATE — add 1 new P0 case: B2B delegated purchasing limit enforcement

coverageGaps.description: COV-2026-03-21-1337 added guest checkout (CHK-066–068) and B2B PO number (CHK-069–072) to suite 04b. Suite 00 only has FRR-028 (standard delivery full flow) and FRR-029 (BOPIS). Guest checkout and B2B PO number workflows are missing from the master suite.
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P1 — guest checkout is a core storefront capability; B2B PO number is mandatory for corporate purchasing
coverageGaps.action: GENERATE — add 2 new P1 cases: Guest checkout flow, B2B checkout with PO number

coverageGaps.description: COV-2026-03-21-1337 added payment decline recovery cases (PAY-DECLINE-006–008) to suite 06. Suite 00 has FRR-038 (invalid card rejected) and FRR-039 (PCI compliance) but no case for the decline → retry with different card flow (BL-CHK-004).
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P0 — payment retry after decline is a core revenue recovery flow
coverageGaps.action: GENERATE — add 1 new P0 case: Payment decline → retry with different card

coverageGaps.description: The following BL-* invariants have NO corresponding case in Suite 00: BL-PRICE-008 (floating-point money arithmetic), BL-ORD-002 (cancellation restores inventory conditionally), BL-ORD-003 (partial fulfillment), BL-AUTH-002 (email verification gate), BL-CAT-002 (virtual catalog inherits physical changes). These are P1-data invariants.
coverageGaps.businessImpact: P1 — data integrity invariants with no master suite coverage
coverageGaps.action: NOTE — BL-PRICE-008 partially covered by FRR-032 (order total formula) and FRR-018 (cart math). BL-ORD-002 and BL-ORD-003 are backend Admin flows (out of scope for Suite 00 frontend master). BL-AUTH-002 and BL-CAT-002 are configuration-dependent — flag as manual items.

phase2_generate.summary: Generated 10 new test cases (FRR-091 to FRR-100) covering the 7 identified gaps. All cases use enriched 15-column agent-native format with proper [NAV]/[ACT]/[WAIT] step tags, [DOM]/[STATE]/[MATH] assertion tags, and Cross_Layer_Checks.

deduplicationCheck.checkedAgainst: All 90 existing FRR-001 to FRR-090 cases
deduplicationCheck.notes: GAP-S00-007 (payment decline retry) is distinct from FRR-038 (invalid card rejected client-side) — FRR-038 tests real-time card format validation before submission; FRR-097 tests server-side decline and retry flow.

generatedCases.title: Checkout — Guest Checkout Full Flow (Anonymous Order)
generatedCases.technique: State Transition (guest vs authenticated checkout gate)

generatedCases.title: Checkout — B2B PO Number Entry and Order Placement
B2B — Delegated Purchasing Limit Triggers Approval Workflow
generatedCases.technique: Boundary Value Analysis (order amount vs. buyer limit)

generatedCases.title: Orders — Return / RMA Request from Order History
Orders — Invoice / PDF Download from Order Detail
Quotes — Expired Quote Cannot Be Converted to Order
Payment — Decline Recovery with Different Card (Retry Flow)
generatedCases.technique: State Transition (payment: Pending → Declined → Retry → Authorized)

generatedCases.title: Admin Impersonation — Login on Behalf and Place Order
generatedCases.technique: Happy Path + Equivalence Partitioning (admin actor vs. customer actor)

generatedCases.title: B2B — Cart Isolation Verified via xAPI (Cross-Layer)
Pricing — Floating-Point Arithmetic: Multi-Item Cart Subtotal Exact Match
generatedCases.technique: Boundary Value Analysis (price precision edge values)

phase3_review.summary: Reviewed all 90 existing cases + 10 new cases (total 100). Primary finding: ALL 90 existing cases are in legacy 11-column format — systematic format gap. Individual case quality is generally good (real UI labels, meaningful assertions), but the missing enrichment columns significantly reduce agent executability.

D1_Structure.finding: Legacy 11-column format across all 90 existing cases. Missing: Business_Rule, Edge_Case_Refs, Test_Data, Cross_Layer_Checks, Failure_Signals, Cleanup. New cases FRR-091–100 use correct 15-column format.

D2_Determinism.finding: Existing cases use numbered prose steps without [NAV]/[ACT]/[WAIT] tags. Agents must infer step types. New cases have proper tags.

D3_Completeness.finding: No Failure_Signals or Cross_Layer_Checks in existing cases. Expected_Result column partially covers assertions but lacks assertion target tags ([DOM], [STATE], [MATH]). No Cleanup column.

D4_Testability.finding: Assertions in Expected_Result are generally falsifiable ('Cart shows 1 line with qty=2, not 2 separate lines'). Priority values are valid (Critical/High/Medium). Steps are logically sequenced.

findings: FRR-034 Steps: hardcoded card number '4622943127013705' — test card data, acceptable for payment tests but should be referenced as {{TEST_CARD_NUMBER}} or noted as environment-specific
FRR-035 Steps: hardcoded card '4111111111111111' — same issue
FRR-036 Steps: hardcoded card '4242424242424242' and env var reference 'DATATRANS_OTP from .env' — inconsistent pattern
FRR-037 Steps: references 'SKYFLOW_VISA, SKYFLOW_EXPIRY, SKYFLOW_CVV from .env' — good pattern (env var names), but should use {{}} syntax

D6_BL_ECL_Coverage.finding: Business_Rule column missing in all 90 existing cases. BL-* references are embedded in Expected_Result text (good for human reading, but agents cannot use them for structured rule lookup). ECL references found in 3 cases (FRR-010, FRR-068, FRR-069). No systematic ECL coverage.

D7_Duplication.finding: No duplicate IDs found. FRR-013 and FRR-072/073 both test XSS/SQL injection — FRR-013 is search-focused, FRR-072/073 are login-focused; not true duplicates. FRR-032 and FRR-040 both verify order totals but from different angles (checkout review vs. confirmation cross-layer); acceptable.

specificFindings.finding: Step 4: 'Attempt to access /account/orders directly' — /account/orders is a valid protected route (not an auth route). This is correct and does not need updating. However, step uses numbered prose without [NAV] tag.
specificFindings.action: AUTO-FIX: Add [NAV] tag in fixed version

specificFindings.finding: Hardcoded card number '4622943127013705' in Steps. Should use {{TEST_CARD_NUMBER}} token or be flagged as environment-specific test data.
specificFindings.action: AUTO-FIX: Note as test card. Payment test cards are environment-specific and acceptable as literals in payment test cases per payment testing conventions. Adding comment in Preconditions.

specificFindings.finding: Hardcoded card '4111111111111111' and expiry '12/2025'. The expiry date 12/2025 may now be expired (current date: 2026-03-21). This card will likely be rejected as expired.
specificFindings.action: AUTO-FIX: Update expiry to 12/2028. Flag for manual verification.

specificFindings.finding: Hardcoded card '4242424242424242' with expiry '12/2025' (expired). References 'DATATRANS_OTP from .env' — inconsistent with {{}} syntax convention.
specificFindings.action: AUTO-FIX: Update expiry to 12/2028. Change env var reference to {{DATATRANS_OTP}}.

specificFindings.finding: References 'SKYFLOW_VISA, SKYFLOW_EXPIRY, SKYFLOW_CVV from .env' using plain text. Should use {{}} syntax.

E2E master case — Expected_Result mixes step markers ('STOREFRONT:', 'EMAIL:', 'ADMIN:') with assertions. This is a good pattern for cross-layer verification but needs to be migrated to the Assertions + Cross_Layer_Checks columns.
All 90 existing cases lack step type tags. Steps are numbered prose ('1. Navigate to...', '2. Click...'). Agents must infer action type from verb.
specificFindings.action: AUTO-FIX: The auto-fix in Phase 4 rewrites the format header and adds enrichment columns. Full step retagging is a MANUAL item — flagged in manualItems.

phase4_fix.summary: Auto-fixes applied: (1) Header migrated to 15-column enriched format. (2) Expired payment card dates updated (FRR-035, FRR-036). (3) Env var syntax standardized for Skyflow (FRR-037) and Datatrans (FRR-036). (4) Business_Rule column populated for all cases where BL-* references exist in Expected_Result. (5) Edge_Case_Refs populated for ECL references. (6) Failure_Signals added (generic signals for all existing cases). (7) Cleanup column added. (8) 10 new cases (FRR-091–100) appended in full 15-column format.

autoFixesApplied.description: CSV header migrated from 11-column legacy to 15-column enriched agent-native format
FRR-035: Updated Authorize.Net test card expiry from 12/2025 to 12/2028
FRR-036: Updated Datatrans test card expiry from 12/2025 to 12/2028; standardized DATATRANS_OTP to {{DATATRANS_OTP}}
FRR-037: Changed 'SKYFLOW_VISA, SKYFLOW_EXPIRY, SKYFLOW_CVV from .env' to '{{SKYFLOW_VISA}}, {{SKYFLOW_EXPIRY}}, {{SKYFLOW_CVV}}'

Business_Rule column populated by extracting BL-* IDs from Expected_Result text in all 90 existing cases. Where Expected_Result had embedded 'BL-XXX' references, these are now promoted to the structured column.

Generic Failure_Signals added to all 90 existing cases: 'Spinner visible >5s, 4xx/5xx on network tab, console TypeError, page redirect to /error'. Cases with payment flows also include 'Payment iframe blank (iframe blocked)'.
10 new test cases FRR-091 to FRR-100 appended to CSV in enriched 15-column format

manualItems.description: All 90 existing cases need step-level retagging: numbered prose steps ('1. Navigate to...', '2. Click...') should be converted to tagged steps ([NAV]/[ACT]/[WAIT]/[ASSERT]). This requires human review of each step to infer correct tag type — auto-tagging risks misclassification of ASSERT steps vs ACT steps.
manualItems.recommendation: Run /qa-test-lifecycle on individual domain batches (e.g., suite 02 for Auth, suite 03 for Catalog) to migrate domain suites first, then re-export master cases from those suites.

manualItems.description: FRR-034 uses hardcoded CyberSource test card '4622943127013705'. This should be stored in .env as TEST_CARD_CYBERSOURCE and referenced as {{TEST_CARD_CYBERSOURCE}} for consistency with other test data patterns.
manualItems.recommendation: Add TEST_CARD_CYBERSOURCE=4622943127013705 to .env template and update FRR-034 steps.

manualItems.description: BL-AUTH-002 (email verification gate) and BL-CAT-002 (virtual catalog inheritance) have no Suite 00 coverage. These require specific store configuration (emailVerificationRequired=true, multi-catalog setup). Determine if QA environment has these configured before adding cases.
manualItems.recommendation: Check QA env store settings via Admin → Settings. If emailVerificationRequired is available, generate a case for FRR-101. If not, add to backlog.

manualItems.description: FRR-083 (Mobile Safari iOS Critical Path) is marked Critical but cannot be executed on Windows (no WebKit support). This case should be delegated to a physical iOS device or BrowserStack. Consider marking as 'Semi-Automated' with note about execution environment.
manualItems.recommendation: Update Automation_Status to 'Manual' with Preconditions noting 'Requires physical iOS device or BrowserStack — not executable on Windows MCP environment'.

manualItems.description: Cross_Layer_Checks column for all 90 existing cases has been left blank (only generic signals added). Each case should have specific API/CONSOLE/NETWORK/ADMIN cross-layer checks added when domain experts migrate the domain suites. This is deferred to per-suite lifecycle runs.
manualItems.recommendation: Address via per-domain /qa-test-lifecycle runs on suites 02, 03, 04a, 04b, 04c, 05, 06, 08, 13.

changes: Business_Rule column populated from embedded BL-* references
Edge_Case_Refs column populated from embedded ECL-* references
Failure_Signals column added with generic signals for all 90 existing cases
FRR-036 Datatrans card expiry updated: 12/2025 → 12/2028; DATATRANS_OTP → {{DATATRANS_OTP}}

FRR091_FRR092.notes: Guest checkout requires store setting createAnonymousOrderEnabled=true

FRR093.notes: Requires org with buyer purchasing limit configured. Admin verification of approval workflow needed.

FRR094_FRR095.notes: Requires placed order in QA environment with return-eligible status

FRR096.notes: Requires quote with past expiry date — may need Admin setup or time manipulation

FRR097.notes: Use decline test card then switch to valid test card — CyberSource primary processor

FRR098.notes: Admin impersonation starts in Admin SPA then switches to storefront context

FRR099.notes: GraphQL xAPI verification — use GraphiQL at {{BACK_URL}}/ui/graphiql

FRR100.notes: Requires products with prices like $X.X95 — may need specific test data setup
